Technologies.Name: Dynamic Balanced Lacing
Technologies.Description: Since its introduction in 2015, Dynamic Balanced Lacing (DBL) has been the cornerstone of Giant WheelSystems. DBL ensures that spokes are set for optimal tension when under dynamic load, resulting in enhanced durability and pedaling efficiency. The enlarged DBL hub flange also increases the spoke bracing angles, which boosts overall torsional/transmission and lateral stiffness for better power transfer and more stable sprinting.

Technologies.Name: Hookless Rim Design
Technologies.Description: Hookless rim design enhances overall durability, offers a wider inner rim width without adding extra weight and also helps create a more round tire shape providing superior grip and handling as well as added sidewall support when cornering. Additionally, without the rim hook to “push” the tire bead inward, hookless rim design offers a more seamless airflow between the tire and the sidewall of the rim, resulting in less drag and improved aero performance when compared to standard hook-type rims.

Technologies.Name: Tubeless System
Technologies.Description: Fewer flat tires. Greater traction. Less rolling resistance and a smoother, faster ride. Giant’s Tubeless System makes it easier than ever to experience all these benefits.

Products.Name: Pursuit Mips Helmet
Features:
CFD-modeled and wind-tunnel-validated aerodynamic design saves 1.66 watts versus the previous model in real-world conditions.
Optimized vent placement and internal airflow channels deliver effective cooling without increasing drag.
Proprietary central dimple geometry smooths airflow and reduces turbulence.
Dual-density EPS with lower hardbody construction enhances impact energy management.
Integrated Mips Air Node Pro provides advanced rotational impact protection.
Five-position Cinch Pro fit system allows precise, race-ready adjustment.
Lightweight, low-profile strap system improves comfort and stability at speed.
Fidlock® magnetic buckle enables fast, secure one-handed fastening.
Integrated eyewear storage securely holds sunglasses when not in use.

Technologies.Name: Virginia Tech 5 Star Rating
Technologies.Description: In collaboration with the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety, the Virginia Tech Helmet lab tests helmets using the STAR evaluation system. The impact tests evaluate a helmet's ability to reduce linear acceleration and rotational velocity of the head resulting from a range of impacts a cyclist might experience. Helmets with more stars provide a reduction in concussion risk for these impacts, with 4 and 5 star ratings being the most highly recommended. Read more.

Technologies.Name: Mips® Air Node Pro
Technologies.Description: Exclusive to Giant and Liv helmets, Mips® Air Node Pro delivers advanced rotational impact protection while maintaining cooling and comfort. Using reengineered materials, COOLMAX® fiber, and smart-flow moisture channels with integrated Drop Zones, it helps keep riders cool and dry during high-intensity efforts. The lightweight system adds virtually no weight or bulk to highly ventilated helmets, while removable pads allow for quick cleaning and easy maintenance.

Technologies.Name: Integrated Eyewear Storage
Technologies.Description: Built-in storage securely holds eyewear when not in use, keeping them stable and easily accessible across varied terrain and riding conditions.

Technologies.Name: Magnetic Fidlock®
Technologies.Description: Magnetic Fidlock® helmet buckles are intuitive, pinch-free and can be opened or closed with one hand, even while wearing gloves.

Technologies.Name: Mips Evolve Core
Technologies.Description: Mips Evolve Core is designed to add protection in helmets against the rotational motion. The rotational motion is a combination of rotational energy (angular velocity) and rotational forces (from angular acceleration) that both affects the brain and increases the risk for minor and severe brain injuries. Mips has been scientifically proven to reduce rotational motion when implemented in a helmet by absorbing and redirecting rotational energies and forces transferred to the brain.

Technologies.Name: Virginia Tech 5 Star Rating
Technologies.Description: In collaboration with the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety, the Virginia Tech Helmet lab tests helmets using the STAR evaluation system. The impact tests evaluate a helmet's ability to reduce linear acceleration and rotational velocity of the head resulting from a range of impacts a cyclist might experience. Helmets with more stars provide a reduction in concussion risk for these impacts, with 4 and 5 star ratings being the most highly recommended. Read more.

Technologies.Name: Mips Evolve Core
Technologies.Description: Mips Evolve Core is designed to add protection in helmets against the rotational motion. The rotational motion is a combination of rotational energy (angular velocity) and rotational forces (from angular acceleration) that both affects the brain and increases the risk for minor and severe brain injuries. Mips has been scientifically proven to reduce rotational motion when implemented in a helmet by absorbing and redirecting rotational energies and forces transferred to the brain.

Technologies.Name: Virginia Tech 4 Star Rating
Technologies.Description: In collaboration with the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety, the Virginia Tech Helmet lab tests helmets using the STAR evaluation system. The impact tests evaluate a helmet's ability to reduce linear acceleration and rotational velocity of the head resulting from a range of impacts a cyclist might experience. Helmets with more stars provide a reduction in concussion risk for these impacts, with 4 and 5 star ratings being the most highly recommended. Read more.

Technologies.Name: Mips Evolve Core
Technologies.Description: Mips Evolve Core is designed to add protection in helmets against the rotational motion. The rotational motion is a combination of rotational energy (angular velocity) and rotational forces (from angular acceleration) that both affects the brain and increases the risk for minor and severe brain injuries. Mips has been scientifically proven to reduce rotational motion when implemented in a helmet by absorbing and redirecting rotational energies and forces transferred to the brain.

Technologies.Name: TransTextura™
Virginia Tech 4 Star Rating
Technologies.Description: In collaboration with the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety, the Virginia Tech Helmet lab tests helmets using the STAR evaluation system. The impact tests evaluate a helmet's ability to reduce linear acceleration and rotational velocity of the head resulting from a range of impacts a cyclist might experience. Helmets with more stars provide a reduction in concussion risk for these impacts, with 4 and 5 star ratings being the most highly recommended. Read more.
